DISCOVERY 301, PLAINTIFFS LELAND EVANS, AND JUAN REYES' MOTION TO COMPEL DEFENDANT DON ANGELO INC.'S VERIFICATIONS TO DEFENDANTS JULY 14 AND JULY 29 AMENDED RESPONSES; REQUEST FOR SANCTIONS AGAINST DEFENDANTS ATTORNEY OF RECORD, GREGORY DE LA PENA. THE UNOPPOSED MOTION TO COMPEL DEFENDANT DON ANGELO INC. TO VERIFY ITS AMENDED RESPONSES TO CERTAIN OF PLAINTIFF LELAND EVANS'S SPECIAL INTERROGATORIES, SET TWO, IS DENIED. THE COURT FINDS SUBSTANTIAL JUSTIFICATION AND DOES NOT AWARD SANCTIONS. (SEE MINI MINITUES FOR COMPLETE RUING) COUNSEL FOR THE PREVAILING PARTY IS REQUIRED TO PREPARE A PROPOSED ORDER WHICH REPEATS VERBATIM THE SUBSTANTIVE PORTION OF THE COURT'S RULING. JUDGE: CHRISTINE VAN AKEN; CLERK: W. TRUPEK; NOT REPORTED (301/CVA)

DEFENDANT SHALL PAY $3,000 TO PLAINTIFF IN SANCTIONS. THIS REDUCED AMOUNT REFLECTS THE RELATIVE SIMPLICITY OF THE MOTION. PAYMENT IS DUE NO LATER THAN 10 COURT DAYS FROM NOTICE OF THIS ORDER. CRC 3.1385 ORDER TO SHOW CAUSE

MASTER CALENDAR JURY
Off Calendar Oct-15-2025 Case Settled - Set For Crc 3.1385 Hrg On Dec-16-25 At 10:30 A.M. In Dept 610

Notice Of Motion And Motion To Compel Defendant Don Angelo Inc.S Verifications To Defendants July 14 And July 29 Amended Responses; Request For Sanctions Against Defendants Attorney Of Record, Gregory De La Pena,
Off Calendar Sep-24-2025 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
No appearances. The Court adopts the tentative ruling as follows:

PLAINTIFFS LELAND EVANS, AND JUAN REYES' Motion To Compel Defendant Don Angelo Inc.'s Verifications To Defendants July 14 And July 29 Amended Responses; Request For Sanctions Against Defendants Attorney Of Record, Gregory De La Pena.

The unopposed motion to compel defendant Don Angelo Inc. to verify its amended responses to certain of plaintiff Leland Evans's special interrogatories, set two, is denied. The court has carefully reviewed the Wiederhold Declaration and it seems plain enough that the verification, which accompanied the July 29, 2025 amended responses, verifies the July 29 amended responses. That leaves the July 14, 2025 amended responses unverified, but upon comparison of the two sets of amended responses, the court finds that all of the factual information contained in the unverified July 14 responses is repeated in the verified July 29 responses.

The court must award sanctions against a party who unsuccessfully makes a motion to compel further responses to interrogatories unless it finds that the party acted with substantial justification or other circumstances make sanctions unjust. (See Code Civ. Proc. 2029.300, subd. (d).) The court finds substantial justification and does not award sanctions.

Counsel for the prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the Court's ruling.

Judge: Christine Van Aken; Clerk: W. Trupek; Not Reported (301/CVA)

Notice Of Motion And Motion To Compel Defendant Don Angelo Inc.'S Further Responses To Plaintiff'S Special Interrogatories, Set Two, Numbers 25, 31, 32 And 49; Request For Sanctions Against Defendant And Its Attorney Of Record, Gregory De La Pena, In The Amount Of $1,5181.42
Off Calendar Jul-15-2025 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES:

Appearances (via Zoom videoconference):
Atty. Mary Catherine Wiederhold, Esq. of Law Office of Mary Catherine Wiederhold (tel: 415-533-0735) for the Plaintiffs.
Atty. Bryan Kurtz, Esq. of De La Pena & Holiday LLP (tel: 415-268-8000) for the Defendants.

Hearing Re: PLAINTIFFS LELAND EVANS, AND JUAN REYES' Motion To Compel Defendant Don Angelo Inc.'s Further Responses To Plaintiff'S Special Interrogatories, Set Two, Numbers 25, 31, 32 And 49; Request For Sanctions Against Defendant And Its Attorney Of Record, Gregory De La Pena, In The Amount Of $1,5181.42.

Matter argued and submitted. Upon conclusion, the Court takes the matter under submission.

Judge: Christine Van Aken; Clerk: V. Da Fonseca; Not reported. (301/CVA)

***** After the hearing, the court signs an order granting Plaintiff's motion to compel (see signed order for complete ruling).*****

Notice Of Motion And Motion To Compel Defendant Don Angelo Inc.S Further Responses To Plaintiffs Form Interrogatories, Set Two, Number 15.1, Section (B) And (C)
Off Calendar Apr-04-2025 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
No appearances. The Court adopts the tentative ruling as follows:

PLAINTIFF LELAND EVANS, JUAN REYES' Motion To Compel Defendant Don Angelo Inc.S Further Responses To Plaintiffs Form Interrogatories, Set Two, Number 15.1, Section (B) And (C)

Plaintiff Leland Evans's motion to compel Defendant Don Angelo Inc. to provide further responses to Plaintiffs form interrogatories, set two, number 15.1, sections (b) and (c) and special interrogatories, set one number 19 within 10 days of notice of the Court's order, is granted. Plaintiff's request for sanctions is granted in part.

The requests at issue are reasonably calculated to lead to the discovery of admissible evidence. (CCP 2017.010.) Plaintiff has shown Defendant's responses are incomplete. Defendant has not demonstrated that any objection has merit. Defendant is obliged to obtain information from the property manager who is its agent and has not demonstrated that information in its property manager's possession is not within its possession, custody, or control. The court orders Defendant to provide an amended response to form interrogatories, set two, number 15.1. section (b) with complete list of witnesses who have knowledge of facts related to each of its affirmative defenses, and for section (c) to identify the documents supporting these affirmative defenses or clearly state it does not have knowledge of any documents supporting these defenses. Defendant shall also provide complete responses to special interrogatories, set one number 19 and give the names of the other tenants at the subject property who complained about Susan Anderson. Plaintiff's meet and confer was reasonable. Defendant must provide a verification. Further responses and verification are due no later than 10 court days from notice of this order.

The court does not find Defendant acted with substantial justification. Defendant shall pay $3,000 to Plaintiff in sanctions. This reduced amount reflects the relative simplicity of the motion. Payment is due no later than 10 court days from notice of this order.

order is signed.

Judge: Christine Van Aken; Clerk: W. Trupek; Not Reported (301/CVA)
